recent_trades.summary: Initiating 5% GE position on bullish earnings momentum and investor attention after GE Vernova blowout results, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: GE shows +0.32 sentiment with headlines highlighting strength in commercial aerospace and 'stock of the day' bullish action; not currently held, technicals near the highs of the recent range, and 5% sizing respects the single-name cap while using proceeds from the UNH exit.

recent_trades.summary: Fully exiting UNH on persistent negative sentiment and 'Forget UnitedHealth' headlines while reallocating capital, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: UNH has been trimmed twice today already; sentiment remains -0.08 with multiple articles suggesting investors look elsewhere. Removing the last 3% position frees capital and removes a drag as technicals show continued underperformance versus the broader healthcare group.

recent_trades.summary: Liquidating Cisco to rotate capital into higher-growth opportunities, as its price action remains largely flat.
recent_trades.reasoning: Despite some positive headline noise, Cisco's technicals show stagnation with a 30-day return of just +0.73% and negative unrealized P/L in the portfolio. Capital is better deployed into Alphabet and Netflix which have stronger, undeniable short-term catalysts.

recent_trades.summary: Liquidating Meta position to free up capital for higher-conviction hyperscaler opportunities amid negative media sentiment.
recent_trades.reasoning: Meta is facing rising capital costs alongside other hyperscalers, but lacks the immediate, massive catalysts seen with Alphabet today. Exiting this small 3% position allows for better capital efficiency elsewhere.

recent_trades.summary: Maxing out Alphabet allocation to 20% following Anthropic's massive $200 billion infrastructure commitment, signaling cloud dominance.
recent_trades.reasoning: The $200 billion commitment from Anthropic is a staggering fundamental catalyst that ensures Google Cloud's long-term revenue growth and competitive edge over peers. I am maximizing this high-conviction position as it represents a permanent shift in AI market share.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a small position in Adobe on its expanded AI push with new Acrobat productivity agent, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Adobe has strong positive sentiment (+0.30) and news about an agentic AI expansion in Acrobat, which could drive growth. The stock is up 1.58% over 30 days but near its 30-day high. Starting a 1% position captures upside without overcommitting late in the day.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Apple from 4.45% to 4% due to negative sentiment from Xi's Taiwan warning, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Apple has negative sentiment driven by geopolitical risk (Taiwan clash warning). The stock remains a core holding, but reducing slightly locks in some gains and reduces event risk exposure. Technicals show tight range near highs.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting Salesforce after Citi cut price target and weak enterprise spending trends, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Salesforce has negative sentiment from a analyst downgrade and slowing enterprise software demand. The position is tiny at 0.49% and not contributing meaningfully. Exiting frees capital for higher-conviction ideas.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Amazon further as it continues to lag other megacap tech despite generally positive AI-related sentiment, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMZN remains one of the weaker charts in the universe, with slightly negative 5-day and 30-day returns and intraday action that is flat to soft versus stronger peers. The layoff headline is not thesis-breaking, but relative strength is clearly inferior to MSFT, NVDA, AVGO and ORCL, so cutting the weight from 10.02% to 8.00% improves portfolio quality.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Walmart again because the upbeat Q1 story is intact but near-term upside looks limited after the recent purchase, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: WMT sentiment is positive, but price is still range-bound near its 30-day high and has not delivered the same follow-through as the leading tech names. Since this was only initiated recently and remains a lower-conviction defensive holding, reducing from 5.00% to 3.00% frees capital for stronger momentum elsewhere.

recent_trades.summary: Adding modestly to Oracle on positive AI-defense partnership news and a strong one-month trend, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: ORCL has the strongest 30-day return in the provided set at +5.72% and the news flow around agentic AI and defense partnerships reinforces the trend. The current holding is only 4.96% and slightly underwater, so a measured add to 7% averages into a constructive setup while staying disciplined on size.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to Broadcom as it presses toward a 30-day high with market-leading chip momentum, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AVGO is one of the strongest names on the board today, up sharply intraday and trading just under its 30-day high of 441.35 with supportive AI/semiconductor headlines. The existing position is only 7.29% of portfolio value, so increasing toward 10% adds to relative strength without concentrating excessively late in the session.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating TJX at 5% target on highest sentiment in universe, Jim Cramer calling it superb and solid Q1 earnings setup, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Retail resilience theme reinforced by WMT UBS forecast and TJX's own quality screen; no current holding, positive 0.39 score and flat technicals near 30D highs offer attractive entry with reallocated capital.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing ORCL to 10% target on 5.8% intraday surge, positive AI defense partnership news and strong technical breakout, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Agentic AI expansion with U.S. Defense not fully reflected in prior buys; 30D high at 200.71 with rising volume supports adding while keeping under 20% cap as conviction leader today.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming UNH from 5% to 3% on continued negative sentiment and 'forget UnitedHealth' headlines, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Recent decision already trimmed this on lack of catalysts; pairing with today's negative news flow to fund retail and AI reallocation while staying under concentration limits.

recent_trades.summary: Fully exiting AMD on negative sentiment and headlines noting Intel's 82% monthly rip while questioning AMD's relative performance, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Geopolitical Taiwan risks and competitive AI chip pressure not yet priced into the mild 30D gain; 5% position can be redeployed to stronger AI momentum names like ORCL that have fresh positive catalysts.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting CRM entirely on negative sentiment, Citi PT cut to $188 and weak enterprise software spending trends, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: News highlights slow revenue growth compared to AI-native firms like Anthropic; small 1% position adds little value and recent decision history shows no fresh positive catalyst since last trim. Frees capital for higher-conviction names near close.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a small WMT position ahead of Q1 earnings with UBS forecasting strong sales growth and very positive sentiment at +0.50.
recent_trades.reasoning: WMT has the highest positive sentiment in the universe today (+0.50) with UBS explicitly forecasting strong Q1 results extending its retail lead. Currently trading near 30-day highs at $132.01. A 4% position provides meaningful exposure without overcommitting near end of day with earnings as catalyst.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 5% position in Intuitive Surgical as an attractive entry point near a multi-year low with emerging agentic AI tailwinds.
recent_trades.reasoning: ISRG is currently trading near its 30-day and multi-year lows, offering an excellent risk/reward ratio. Recent media highlighting the company as a top agentic AI play in healthcare provides a compelling narrative for a turnaround and diversification away from big tech.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 5% position in Adobe to capture upside from its newly announced Acrobat Productivity Agent and expanding agentic AI capabilities.
recent_trades.reasoning: Adobe's move into agentic AI provides a strong fundamental growth catalyst that differentiates it from hardware peers. Rotating capital from Apple into Adobe allows us to maintain tech exposure while shifting away from supply chain risks into software-driven AI upside.

recent_trades.summary: Liquidating Apple entirely due to new headlines highlighting severe geopolitical risks over Taiwan that threaten its heavily concentrated supply chain.
recent_trades.reasoning: While earlier analyst upgrades were promising, the new warning from Xi regarding a potential clash over Taiwan introduces unacceptable supply chain and geopolitical risks. Exiting the 10% position to protect capital before the market fully digests the implications.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting SBUX — down -1.1% today hitting 30-day lows at $106.64 despite the earlier upgrade; price action contradicts the thesis.
recent_trades.reasoning: SBUX is at its 30-day low despite the TD Cowen upgrade we bought into earlier. The -0.83% 5-day and -1.09% 30-day trends suggest the upgrade isn't gaining traction. Small position at 3% with a -0.88% unrealized loss — better to cut and redeploy.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting T-Mobile due to negative sentiment and lack of near-term catalysts, reallocating to cash.
recent_trades.reasoning: TMUS has negative sentiment and is down 2.81% unrealized. The FCC approval for Verizon spectrum and formation of a satellite JV with AT&T and Verizon do not provide a clear catalyst for TMUS. With geopolitical risks from Xi's Taiwan comments, reducing exposure in a weak holding is prudent.

recent_trades.summary: Slightly trimming Walmart after the recent buy because positive Q1 sentiment is already reflected and upside follow-through has been modest, confidence 5/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: WMT sentiment is strong, but the stock itself is still flat-to-soft across 1D, 5D, and 30D windows and remains below its 30-day high. This is a modest rebalance rather than a negative call, freeing a bit of capital from a defensive name to fund a higher-momentum opportunity into the close.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Amazon because the stock is lagging other megacap tech leaders and the layoff headline tempers otherwise constructive AI sentiment, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMZN is slightly negative intraday and down over the last 30 days, underperforming stronger AI-linked peers already held in the portfolio. Since cash is nearly zero, trimming a relatively large 12.9% position creates room to reallocate toward stronger trend names without exiting a still-constructive core holding.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming SBUX from 3.97% to 2% as the position remains slightly underwater (-0.48%) despite the TD Cowen upgrade catalyst already being priced in from our recent purchase.

The positive sentiment (+0.17) and margin recovery thesis are intact but not yet showing in price action. Trimming to 2% reduces risk on a thesis that needs more time to play out, freeing capital for higher-conviction opportunities.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming UNH from 13.8% to 10% as negative sentiment -0.08 and headlines suggesting investors look elsewhere reduce conviction in the oversized position.

recent_trades.reasoning: ORCL is the top performer in the universe today at +5.7%, and the defense AI partnership headline is a genuine fundamental catalyst — government contracts provide durable revenue. The 30-day performance of +5.74% confirms sustained momentum. Initiating a modest 5% position as a new catalyst-driven trade distinct from prior decisions.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating WMT at 5% target on +0.50 sentiment, UBS strong Q1 forecast, and retail sector resilience, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: WMT is not in the current portfolio and has the highest sentiment reading with clear fundamental tailwinds from expected sales growth; this diversifies the heavy tech concentration slightly while the 30D chart shows stable uptrend near highs.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Oracle weight to 12% to capitalize on its expanding agentic AI push and lucrative new U.S. Defense partnerships.
recent_trades.reasoning: Oracle is showing strong relative strength, up 5.74% over the last 30 days. The recent announcement of its expansion into U.S. Defense partnerships for its AI platform is a massive fundamental catalyst that justifies upsizing our existing winning position.

recent_trades.reasoning: Walmart offers excellent defensive characteristics in a market facing geopolitical uncertainties. Positive sentiment and an upbeat Q1 preview from UBS regarding its retail lead and sales growth make this an attractive entry point for capital preservation and steady growth.

recent_trades.reasoning: UNH has performed well with a 9% unrealized gain, but recent media sentiment has turned slightly negative with headlines suggesting better alternatives in the space. Liquidating this position locks in profits and provides liquidity for higher-conviction opportunities.

recent_trades.summary: Liquidating AMD position entirely due to escalating geopolitical tensions over Taiwan that threaten its TSMC-dependent supply chain.
recent_trades.reasoning: The headline indicating a potential clash over Taiwan poses a severe macroeconomic risk to fabless semiconductor companies. AMD lacks the monopolistic pricing power of Nvidia to weather such a storm, making it a prudent sale to protect capital and reduce risk.

The stock has room to run given the secular AI theme and defense tailwinds. Increasing allocation to 6% is within risk limits and funded by trimming QCOM.

recent_trades.reasoning: QCOM has negative sentiment from recent headlines (Taiwan clash warning) and a 4.44% unrealized loss. Reducing exposure mitigates risk from potential China-related disruptions, while the proceeds can be allocated to ORCL with a stronger catalyst.

recent_trades.summary: Adding slightly to NVDA as it continues to lead large-cap tech on strong momentum and positive AI-related sentiment, confidence 7/10.

This is only a modest increase from the current 12.03% weight to avoid concentration risk and because the name is already extended, but it remains one of the clearest intraday leaders to absorb the GE proceeds.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting the tiny GE position because it is too small to matter and the stock remains below its 30-day high despite positive aerospace headlines, confidence 6/10.

recent_trades.reasoning: The news that Anthropic is committing $200 billion to Google's infrastructure is an unprecedented hyperscaler deal that cements GOOGL's dominance in the AI arms race. This fundamentally alters their cloud revenue trajectory, justifying a massive overweight position to capture the upside.

recent_trades.reasoning: AbbVie has served as a stable defensive hold, but the current market environment overwhelmingly favors AI and cloud infrastructure. Exiting this position provides the necessary liquidity to aggressively scale into Alphabet.

recent_trades.summary: Liquidating American Express to free up capital for high-conviction AI infrastructure opportunities.
recent_trades.reasoning: With AXP lagging in momentum and showing a slight unrealized loss, capital is better deployed into hyperscalers that are securing massive infrastructure deals. This is a purely strategic reallocation to fund aggressive tech buys.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing ORCL to 9% target on 4.6% intraday surge, positive +0.31 sentiment, and new U.S. defense AI partnership news.
recent_trades.reasoning: Chart shows clear breakout above recent highs with strong volume, reinforcing earlier AI momentum; recent add was hours ago but fresh headlines provide new catalyst, conviction high as it fits core tech theme without exceeding position limits.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating ORCL at 4% — surging +4.6% today on U.S. defense AI partnership expansion with strong positive sentiment, confidence 7/10.

The stock has gained +4.58% over 30 days showing strong trend. Defense AI is a durable revenue driver. Entering at 4% given the already elevated intraday move limits upside risk.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming CRM from 4% to 2% after Citi cut price target from $200 to $188 citing weak enterprise software spending, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Citi's price target cut is a meaningful negative catalyst for CRM, which was already down slightly in our portfolio. The weak enterprise software spending narrative could pressure the stock further. Reducing exposure while maintaining a small position in case Agentforce momentum offsets the concern.

recent_trades.summary: Starting a GE position on constructive aerospace news and improving sentiment after a pullback into the lower end of its recent range, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: GE is weak on a 30-day basis, but sentiment is positive and aerospace-related commentary suggests improving fundamental backdrop after recent pressure. This looks more like an early rotation candidate than a confirmed momentum leader, so I would size it modestly at 5%.

universe.description: 79-name universe: 75 top U.S. mega/large-cap stocks with full GICS sector coverage plus 4 commodity ETFs (gold, silver, oil, copper).

mvp_trade.summary: Trimming QCOM from 5.7% to 3% to free capital for higher-conviction AI plays; position remains underwater at -5.1% despite positive news today.

market_brief.brief: Welcome. U.S. equities: S&P +0.77%, Nasdaq +0.88%, Dow +0.75%. Technology led sectors (+1.80%) while Commodities lagged (-1.39%). Grok 4.20 (Reasoning) led the day at +1.84%. 6 of 6 models green on the day. Cumulative leader: DeepSeek Reasoner at +22.53%. Key headline: Senate confirms new Fed chair as Trump allies warn rate cuts may have to wait - The Washington Post — market reaction muted.
market_brief.key_moves: CSCO +13.4% (held by 5/6) | QCOM -6.1% (held by 1/6) | AVGO +5.5% (held by 5/6)
market_brief.macro_sentence: Key headline: Senate confirms new Fed chair as Trump allies warn rate cuts may have to wait - The Washington Post — market reaction muted.
